chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1295 of 2010 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Before; Mr. Justice Muhammad Shafi Siddiqui Mr. Justice Irshad Ali Shah. 21.04.2022 Mr. Ghulam Nabi Jarwar, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Ayaz Ali Rajpar, Assistant Advocate General, Sindh. == The petitioner by way of instant petition sought for her appointment in Civil Service on deceased quota basis for the reason that her father being employee of Education Department as H.S.T died during service on 05.07.2007. As per comments filed by Executive District Officer Mirpurkhas, the petitioner could not be appointed as she left the process of her appointment incomplete. No person of prudent mind would leave the process of her appointment in Civil Service incomplete before Administrative Department and then would approach the High Court, for the needful. The petitioner has made out a case for her appointment in Civil Service, on deceased quota basis; such right could not be denied to her under any pretext. Consequently, the respondents are directed to appoint the petitioner in Civil Service, on deceased quota basis, against a suitable post, after observing all the codal formalities, in accordance with law, preferably within three months, hereinafter. Instant constitutional petition is disposed of accordingly. J U D G E J U D G E Muhammad Danish*
